raw:
{
  "judge_id": "J123",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.1", "R1.4", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 5,
    "R1.4": 5,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 5
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The agent identifies the load-bearing concept (instruction-following vs. raw translation quality) and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.",
    "R1.4": "The agent maps against several nearest works (INSTRUCTSCORE, WMT general/terminology shared tasks, MT-GenEval) and identifies sharp delta.",
    "RX.1": "The agent flags uncertainty for specific claims (prior art, contamination) and marks confidence in its own critique.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers exactly the agreed active set with no drift.",
    "RX.4": "No regressions; the agent identifies harmful patterns in the original (e.g., overclaims, methodological gaps).",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.9,
    "R1.4": 0.8,
    "RX.1": 0.85,
    "RX.3": 0.95,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.9
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: The agent identifies the load-bearing concept (instruction-following vs. raw translation quality) and proposes a reframing that survives prior-work contrast.
rationale.R1.4: The agent maps against several nearest works (INSTRUCTSCORE, WMT general/terminology shared tasks, MT-GenEval) and identifies sharp delta.
rationale.RX.1: The agent flags uncertainty for specific claims (prior art, contamination) and marks confidence in its own critique.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed active set with no drift.
rationale.RX.4: No regressions; the agent identifies harmful patterns in the original (e.g., overclaims, methodological gaps).
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason.
